body_txt: It's big, bulky, loud, and kicks like a mule. It is not always practical , but nonetheless looks really, really cool. It may sport a nickel finish, or better yet, gold. It could even have a name. It's the Hand Cannon . And its stopping power goes WAY beyond the standard sidearm. Magnum-caliber handguns and pistols that use rifle cartridges comprise most of the Hand Cannon trope due to their superior ballistics. Sawed-Off Shotguns /rifles, grenade launchers , and fictional pistols that fire especially powerful ammo or destructive laser beams are also considered. A large pistol may not be a hand cannon if it's chambered for a weak caliber, or simply not that strong. Back in The '70s , a hand cannon was a .357 or .44 Magnum revolver, like the Colt Python or the Smith & Wesson Model 29. In modern times, bigger and stronger revolvers like the Raging Bull in .454 Casull and the extremely powerful Smith & Wesson Model 500 in .500 S&W Magnum reign supreme. Eventually, semi-automatics firing high power rounds appeared to compete with magnum revolvers. The crown of most ridiculously oversized semi-auto handgun goes to the Desert Eagle in .50 Action Express. Other Magnum semi-automatics also exist, but the Deagle is king with the Rule of Cool . note In Video Games , the Desert Eagle is used as a symbol of just how badass the military is, never minding the fact that a huge gun like a Desert Eagle would never be realistically used by any unit, Special Ops or otherwise, because they're just too heavy and loud — the number of real units that actually issue the Desert Eagle can be counted on one hand, with three fingers left over. As of 2025, two nations have forces, both of which are spec ops, that have been issued the Desert Eagle - GROM in Poland, and Portugal's GOE . The Desert Eagle is a particularly popular choice in Hollywood when arming characters played by people the size of, say, Arnold Schwarzenegger , because it's one of the few production firearms that doesn't look like a dinky little pocket pistol when held by someone with hands that big; and in the significantly smaller hands of someone, well, significantly smaller, its significant bulk translates, visually, to "powerful" in a way that much more practical (and much less expensive) firearms just don't. That said, unless you're choosing a weapon for how it's going to look on film, it's worth noting that a .22 and a .50AE are both capable of making the same size hole — about six feet long and nine feet deep . You know you're dealing with this if a character says something along the lines of, "Wow, that's a really big gun," or "He's Compensating for Something ". Sometimes, the user's hands are barely big enough to support the weapon. In egregious cases they're fired one-handed or even dual-wielded — try this in Real Life without the proper strength and experience and you'll end up with a gun launching itself out of your hand (possibly smacking you in the face), and a bullet going nowhere where it's supposed to . Hand Cannons can deal a world of hurt to the unlucky SOB shot by one: inflicting a One-Hit Kill if said cannon is powerful enough, and even knocking them back a foot or two . Attach a scope to one and it becomes a Sniper Pistol , effective at long distances. And if you're really going all-out, load the thing with Explosive Bullets . The Hero who uses the giant handgun as a primary weapon will sometimes (but not always ) display Improbable Aiming Skills to make up for the lack of ammo capacity. In rare cases said character is superhuman , supernatural or cybernetically enhanced in order to compensate for the weapon's weight and recoil. In a series where the heroes battle inhuman or robotic enemies that shrug off small caliber bullets, hand cannons can prove to be absolutely practical weapons. A subtrope of Bigger Is Better . Differs from BFG in that the Hand Cannon is large for a handgun , while the BFG pushes the limit of what a single person can wield at all. Not to be confused with Arm Cannon , which is literally a cannon on the arm. While the Hand Cannon is a special handgun powerful by design, the Punch-Packing Pistol is a standard sidearm that deals an absurd amount of damage, stopping just short of Hand Cannon levels.
